<path>/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/scripts/orphan-mcp-sweeper.sh</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: #!/usr/bin/env bash
2: # Sweep stale MCP helper processes and temporary dispatch artifacts.
3: #
4: # Usage:
5: #   orphan-mcp-sweeper.sh [--dry-run] [--verbose] [--log-path PATH]
6: #
7: # Defaults:
8: #   ORPHAN_AGE_MIN_SEC=300
9: #   ORPHAN_TMP_AGE_HOURS=24
10: #   ORPHAN_SWEEPER_LOG_MAX_BYTES=10485760
11: #   ORPHAN_SWEEPER_LOG_PATH=$HOME/.local/share/orphan-sweeper.log
12: 
13: set -euo pipefail
14: 
15: DRY_RUN=false
16: VERBOSE=false
17: AGE_MIN_SEC="${ORPHAN_AGE_MIN_SEC:-300}"
18: TMP_AGE_HOURS="${ORPHAN_TMP_AGE_HOURS:-24}"
19: LOG_PATH="${ORPHAN_SWEEPER_LOG_PATH:-${HOME:-/tmp}/.local/share/orphan-sweeper.log}"
20: LOG_EXPLICIT=false
21: LOG_MAX_BYTES="${ORPHAN_SWEEPER_LOG_MAX_BYTES:-10485760}"
22: EXIT_CODE=0
23: 
24: candidate_pids=()
25: candidate_ppids=()
26: candidate_classes=()
27: candidate_ages=()
28: candidate_etimes=()
29: candidate_rsses=()
30: candidate_commands=()
31: 
32: kill_pids=()
33: kill_classes=()
34: kill_ages=()
35: kill_etimes=()
36: kill_rsses=()
37: kill_commands=()
38: 
39: min_classes=()
40: min_ages=()
41: min_pids=()
42: 
43: session_tree_pids=()
44: 
45: usage() {
46:   cat <<'EOF'
47: Usage: orphan-mcp-sweeper.sh [--dry-run] [--verbose] [--log-path PATH]
48: 
49: Options:
50:   --dry-run        Log matching kill/remove actions without mutating anything.
51:   --verbose        Log preserve decisions as well as actions.
52:   --log-path PATH  Append logs to PATH. Default: ~/.local/share/orphan-sweeper.log.
53:   -h, --help       Show this help.
54: EOF
55: }
56: 
57: while [ "$#" -gt 0 ]; do
58:   case "$1" in
59:     --dry-run)
60:       DRY_RUN=true
61:       ;;
62:     --verbose)
63:       VERBOSE=true
64:       ;;
65:     --log-path)
66:       if [ "$#" -lt 2 ]; then
67:         echo "ERROR: --log-path requires a path" >&2
68:         exit 1
69:       fi
70:       LOG_PATH="$2"
71:       LOG_EXPLICIT=true
72:       shift
73:       ;;
74:     -h|--help)
75:       usage
76:       exit 0
77:       ;;
78:     *)
79:       echo "ERROR: unknown argument: $1" >&2
80:       usage >&2
81:       exit 1
82:       ;;
83:   esac
84:   shift
85: done
86: 
87: is_positive_int() {
88:   case "$1" in
89:     ''|*[!0-9]*) return 1 ;;
90:     *) [ "$1" -gt 0 ] ;;
91:   esac
92: }
93: 
94: if ! is_positive_int "$AGE_MIN_SEC"; then
95:   AGE_MIN_SEC=300
96: fi
97: if ! is_positive_int "$TMP_AGE_HOURS"; then
98:   TMP_AGE_HOURS=24
99: fi
100: if ! is_positive_int "$LOG_MAX_BYTES"; then
101:   LOG_MAX_BYTES=10485760
102: fi
103: 
104: should_append_log_file() {
105:   [ "$LOG_EXPLICIT" = true ] || [ -t 1 ]
106: }
107: 
108: rotate_log_if_needed() {
109:   should_append_log_file || return 0
110:   [ -n "$LOG_PATH" ] || return 0
111:   [ -f "$LOG_PATH" ] || return 0
112: 
113:   local size
114:   size="$(wc -c < "$LOG_PATH" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)"
115:   is_positive_int "$size" || size=0
116:   [ "$size" -le "$LOG_MAX_BYTES" ] && return 0
117: 
118:   mv -f "$LOG_PATH.2" "$LOG_PATH.3" 2>/dev/null || true
119:   mv -f "$LOG_PATH.1" "$LOG_PATH.2" 2>/dev/null || true
120:   mv -f "$LOG_PATH" "$LOG_PATH.1" 2>/dev/null || true
121: }
122: 
123: emit() {
124:   local line
125:   line="$(date '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z') $*"
126:   printf '%s\n' "$line"
127:   if should_append_log_file && [ -n "$LOG_PATH" ]; then
128:     mkdir -p "$(dirname "$LOG_PATH")" 2>/dev/null || true
129:     printf '%s\n' "$line" >> "$LOG_PATH" 2>/dev/null || true
130:   fi
131: }
132: 
133: log_action() {
134:   if [ "$DRY_RUN" = true ]; then
135:     emit "[DRY-RUN] $*"
136:   else
137:     emit "$*"
138:   fi
139: }
140: 
141: log_verbose() {
142:   [ "$VERBOSE" = true ] || return 0
143:   emit "$*"
144: }
145: 
146: etime_to_seconds() {
147:   local raw="$1"
148:   local days=0
149:   local rest="$raw"
150:   local h=0
151:   local m=0
152:   local s=0
153:   local a b c
154: 
155:   case "$rest" in
156:     *-*)
157:       days="${rest%%-*}"
158:       rest="${rest#*-}"
159:       ;;
160:   esac
161: 
162:   IFS=: read -r a b c <<EOF
163: $rest
164: EOF
165:   if [ -n "${c:-}" ]; then
166:     h="$a"
167:     m="$b"
168:     s="$c"
169:   elif [ -n "${b:-}" ]; then
170:     m="$a"
171:     s="$b"
172:   else
173:     s="$a"
174:   fi
175: 
176:   days="${days#0}"; h="${h#0}"; m="${m#0}"; s="${s#0}"
177:   days="${days:-0}"; h="${h:-0}"; m="${m:-0}"; s="${s:-0}"
178:   printf '%s\n' $((days * 86400 + h * 3600 + m * 60 + s))
179: }
180: 
181: classify_command() {
182:   local cmd="$1"
183:   case "$cmd" in
184:     *"mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs"*) printf '%s\n' "mk-spec-memory-launcher"; return 0 ;;
185:     *"mk-skill-advisor-launcher.cjs"*) printf '%s\n' "mk-skill-advisor-launcher"; return 0 ;;
186:     *"mk-code-index-launcher.cjs"*) printf '%s\n' "mk-code-index-launcher"; return 0 ;;
187:     *"system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/context-server.js"*) printf '%s\n' "spec-memory-context-server"; return 0 ;;
188:     *"system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/dist/"*"advisor-server.js"*) printf '%s\n' "skill-advisor-server"; return 0 ;;
189:     *"system-code-graph/mcp_server/dist/index.js"*) printf '%s\n' "code-graph-server"; return 0 ;;
190:     *"mcp-code-mode/mcp_server/dist/index.js"*) printf '%s\n' "mcp-code-mode"; return 0 ;;
191:     *"npm exec @taazkareem/clickup-mcp-server"*) printf '%s\n' "clickup-mcp-wrapper"; return 0 ;;
192:     *"clickup-mcp-server"*) printf '%s\n' "clickup-mcp-server"; return 0 ;;
193:     *"npm exec @modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking"*)
194:       printf '%s\n' "sequential-thinking-wrapper"
195:       return 0
196:       ;;
197:     *"@modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking"*|*"server-sequential-thinking"*)
198:       printf '%s\n' "sequential-thinking-server"
199:       return 0
200:       ;;
201:   esac
202:   return 1
203: }
204: 
205: class_index() {
206:   local class="$1"
207:   local i
208:   for i in "${!min_classes[@]}"; do
209:     if [ "${min_classes[$i]}" = "$class" ]; then
210:       printf '%s\n' "$i"
211:       return 0
212:     fi
213:   done
214:   return 1
215: }
216: 
217: remember_min_for_class() {
218:   local class="$1"
219:   local age="$2"
220:   local pid="$3"
221:   local idx
222:   idx="$(class_index "$class" 2>/dev/null || true)"
223:   if [ -z "$idx" ]; then
224:     min_classes+=("$class")
225:     min_ages+=("$age")
226:     min_pids+=("$pid")
227:     return
228:   fi
229:   if [ "$age" -lt "${min_ages[$idx]}" ]; then
230:     min_ages[$idx]="$age"
231:     min_pids[$idx]="$pid"
232:   fi
233: }
234: 
235: freshest_pid_for_class() {
236:   local class="$1"
237:   local idx
238:   idx="$(class_index "$class" 2>/dev/null || true)"
239:   [ -n "$idx" ] || return 1
240:   printf '%s\n' "${min_pids[$idx]}"
241: }
242: 
243: freshest_age_for_class() {
244:   local class="$1"
245:   local idx
246:   idx="$(class_index "$class" 2>/dev/null || true)"
247:   [ -n "$idx" ] || return 1
248:   printf '%s\n' "${min_ages[$idx]}"
249: }
250: 
251: pid_in_list() {
252:   local needle="$1"
253:   shift
254:   local item
255:   for item in "$@"; do
256:     [ "$item" = "$needle" ] && return 0
257:   done
258:   return 1
259: }
260: 
261: append_descendants() {
262:   local parent="$1"
263:   local child
264:   while read -r child; do
265:     [ -n "$child" ] || continue
266:     if [ "${#session_tree_pids[@]}" -eq 0 ] || ! pid_in_list "$child" "${session_tree_pids[@]}"; then
267:       session_tree_pids+=("$child")
268:       append_descendants "$child"
269:     fi
270:   done <<EOF
271: $(pgrep -P "$parent" 2>/dev/null || true)
272: EOF
273: }
274: 
275: # Build the live operator-session process tree across ALL AI CLI runtimes, not just
276: # Claude Code. Any MCP helper descended from a live claude / opencode / codex
277: # session belongs to an active operator session and must be preserved — most importantly
278: # an operator's `opencode run`, which previously fell outside the Claude-only tree and
279: # got swept after ORPHAN_AGE_MIN_SEC (a hard-rule violation).
280: build_session_trees() {
281:   local pid
282:   while read -r pid; do
283:     [ -n "$pid" ] || continue
284:     if [ "${#session_tree_pids[@]}" -eq 0 ] || ! pid_in_list "$pid" "${session_tree_pids[@]}"; then
285:       session_tree_pids+=("$pid")
286:       append_descendants "$pid"
287:     fi
288:   done <<EOF
289: $(pgrep -f '(^|/)(claude|opencode|codex)( |$)|Claude Code' 2>/dev/null || true)
290: EOF
291: }
292: 
293: has_non_mcp_listener() {
294:   local pid="$1"
295:   local line
296:   while read -r line; do
297:     case "$line" in
298:       *LISTEN*)
299:         return 0
300:         ;;
301:     esac
302:   done <<EOF
303: $(lsof -nP -a -p "$pid" -iTCP -sTCP:LISTEN 2>/dev/null || true)
304: EOF
305:   return 1
306: }
307: 
308: # The MCP daemons bridge sibling sessions over a UNIX socket (daemon-ipc.sock),
309: # NOT TCP, so has_non_mcp_listener never covers them. Under default-on daemon
310: # re-election a still-serving daemon is reparented to pid 1 and falls outside every
311: # live-session tree — yet killing it drops the transport for any concurrent live
312: # session bridged to it. A daemon actively serving connections holds MORE THAN ONE
313: # unix-socket FD on its daemon-ipc.sock (the listener + one per live peer); a bare
314: # listener with no clients holds exactly one. Treat >1 as "in use" and preserve it.
315: has_live_ipc_socket_connection() {
316:   local pid="$1"
317:   local count
318:   count="$(lsof -nP -a -p "$pid" -U 2>/dev/null | grep -c 'daemon-ipc\.sock' || true)"
319:   [ -n "$count" ] || count=0
320:   [ "$count" -gt 1 ] 2>/dev/null
321: }
322: 
323: preserve_reason() {
324:   local pid="$1"
325:   local class="$2"
326:   local age="$3"
327:   local cmd="$4"
328:   local freshest_pid
329:   local freshest_age
330: 
331:   # Operator-owned interactive/dispatch sessions across runtimes. These commands name a
332:   # session the operator is actively running; their MCP helpers must never be swept.
333:   # build_session_trees covers descendants via process ancestry; these patterns also
334:   # catch the session's own root process whatever its descendants look like.
335:   case "$cmd" in
336:     *"opencode run"*) printf '%s\n' "operator-opencode-preserve"; return 0 ;;
337:     *"codex exec"*) printf '%s\n' "operator-codex-preserve"; return 0 ;;
338:     *"ollama runner"*|*"ollama serve"*) printf '%s\n' "ollama-preserve"; return 0 ;;
339:   esac
340: 
341:   if [ "${#session_tree_pids[@]}" -gt 0 ] && pid_in_list "$pid" "${session_tree_pids[@]}"; then
342:     printf '%s\n' "live-session-tree"
343:     return 0
344:   fi
345: 
346:   freshest_pid="$(freshest_pid_for_class "$class" 2>/dev/null || true)"
347:   freshest_age="$(freshest_age_for_class "$class" 2>/dev/null || echo 999999)"
348:   if [ "$pid" = "$freshest_pid" ] && [ "$freshest_age" -lt "$AGE_MIN_SEC" ]; then
349:     printf '%s\n' "freshest-young-instance"
350:     return 0
351:   fi
352: 
353:   if has_non_mcp_listener "$pid"; then
354:     printf '%s\n' "active-non-mcp-tcp-listener"
355:     return 0
356:   fi
357: 
358:   if has_live_ipc_socket_connection "$pid"; then
359:     printf '%s\n' "active-ipc-socket-connection"
360:     return 0
361:   fi
362: 
363:   return 1
364: }
365: 
366: scan_processes() {
367:   local pid ppid etime rss stat command class age
368:   while read -r pid ppid etime rss stat command; do
369:     [ -n "${pid:-}" ] || continue
370:     class="$(classify_command "${command:-}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
371:     [ -n "$class" ] || continue
372:     age="$(etime_to_seconds "$etime")"
373:     candidate_pids+=("$pid")
374:     candidate_ppids+=("$ppid")
375:     candidate_classes+=("$class")
376:     candidate_ages+=("$age")
377:     candidate_etimes+=("$etime")
378:     candidate_rsses+=("$rss")
379:     candidate_commands+=("${command:-}")
380:     remember_min_for_class "$class" "$age" "$pid"
381:   done <<EOF
382: $(ps -axo pid=,ppid=,etime=,rss=,stat=,command=)
383: EOF
384: }
385: 
386: select_kill_candidates() {
387:   local i pid class age etime rss cmd reason
388:   for i in "${!candidate_pids[@]}"; do
389:     pid="${candidate_pids[$i]}"
390:     class="${candidate_classes[$i]}"
391:     age="${candidate_ages[$i]}"
392:     etime="${candidate_etimes[$i]}"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Sweep stale MCP helper processes and temporary dispatch artifacts.
#
# Usage:
#   orphan-mcp-sweeper.sh [--dry-run] [--verbose] [--log-path PATH]
#
# Defaults:
#   ORPHAN_AGE_MIN_SEC=300
#   ORPHAN_TMP_AGE_HOURS=24
#   ORPHAN_SWEEPER_LOG_MAX_BYTES=10485760
#   ORPHAN_SWEEPER_LOG_PATH=$HOME/.local/share/orphan-sweeper.log

set -euo pipefail

DRY_RUN=false
VERBOSE=false
AGE_MIN_SEC="${ORPHAN_AGE_MIN_SEC:-300}"
TMP_AGE_HOURS="${ORPHAN_TMP_AGE_HOURS:-24}"
LOG_PATH="${ORPHAN_SWEEPER_LOG_PATH:-${HOME:-/tmp}/.local/share/orphan-sweeper.log}"
LOG_EXPLICIT=false

display.text:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Sweep stale MCP helper processes and temporary dispatch artifacts.
#
# Usage:
#   orphan-mcp-sweeper.sh [--dry-run] [--verbose] [--log-path PATH]
#
# Defaults:
#   ORPHAN_AGE_MIN_SEC=300
#   ORPHAN_TMP_AGE_HOURS=24
#   ORPHAN_SWEEPER_LOG_MAX_BYTES=10485760
#   ORPHAN_SWEEPER_LOG_PATH=$HOME/.local/share/orphan-sweeper.log

set -euo pipefail

DRY_RUN=false
VERBOSE=false
AGE_MIN_SEC="${ORPHAN_AGE_MIN_SEC:-300}"
TMP_AGE_HOURS="${ORPHAN_TMP_AGE_HOURS:-24}"
LOG_PATH="${ORPHAN_SWEEPER_LOG_PATH:-${HOME:-/tmp}/.local/share/orphan-sweeper.log}"
LOG_EXPLICIT=false
LOG_MAX_BYTES="${ORPHAN_SWEEPER_LOG_MAX_BYTES:-10485760}"
EXIT_CODE=0

candidate_pids=()
candidate_ppids=()
candidate_classes=()
candidate_ages=()
candidate_etimes=()
candidate_rsses=()
candidate_commands=()

kill_pids=()
kill_classes=()
kill_ages=()
kill_etimes=()
kill_rsses=()
kill_commands=()

min_classes=()
min_ages=()
min_pids=()

session_tree_pids=()

usage() {
  cat <<'EOF'
Usage: orphan-mcp-sweeper.sh [--dry-run] [--verbose] [--log-path PATH]

Options:
  --dry-run        Log matching kill/remove actions without mutating anything.
  --verbose        Log preserve decisions as well as actions.
  --log-path PATH  Append logs to PATH. Default: ~/.local/share/orphan-sweeper.log.
  -h, --help       Show this help.
EOF
}

while [ "$#" -gt 0 ]; do
  case "$1" in
    --dry-run)
      DRY_RUN=true
      ;;
    --verbose)
      VERBOSE=true
      ;;
    --log-path)
      if [ "$#" -lt 2 ]; then
        echo "ERROR: --log-path requires a path" >&2
        exit 1
      fi
      LOG_PATH="$2"
      LOG_EXPLICIT=true
      shift
      ;;
    -h|--help)
      usage
      exit 0
      ;;
    *)
      echo "ERROR: unknown argument: $1" >&2
      usage >&2
      exit 1
      ;;
  esac
  shift
done

is_positive_int() {
  case "$1" in
    ''|*[!0-9]*) return 1 ;;
    *) [ "$1" -gt 0 ] ;;
  esac
}

if ! is_positive_int "$AGE_MIN_SEC"; then
  AGE_MIN_SEC=300
fi
if ! is_positive_int "$TMP_AGE_HOURS"; then
  TMP_AGE_HOURS=24
fi
if ! is_positive_int "$LOG_MAX_BYTES"; then
  LOG_MAX_BYTES=10485760
fi

should_append_log_file() {
  [ "$LOG_EXPLICIT" = true ] || [ -t 1 ]
}

rotate_log_if_needed() {
  should_append_log_file || return 0
  [ -n "$LOG_PATH" ] || return 0
  [ -f "$LOG_PATH" ] || return 0

  local size
  size="$(wc -c < "$LOG_PATH" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)"
  is_positive_int "$size" || size=0
  [ "$size" -le "$LOG_MAX_BYTES" ] && return 0

  mv -f "$LOG_PATH.2" "$LOG_PATH.3" 2>/dev/null || true
  mv -f "$LOG_PATH.1" "$LOG_PATH.2" 2>/dev/null || true
  mv -f "$LOG_PATH" "$LOG_PATH.1" 2>/dev/null || true
}

emit() {
  local line
  line="$(date '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z') $*"
  printf '%s\n' "$line"
  if should_append_log_file && [ -n "$LOG_PATH" ]; then
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$LOG_PATH")" 2>/dev/null || true
    printf '%s\n' "$line" >> "$LOG_PATH" 2>/dev/null || true
  fi
}

log_action() {
  if [ "$DRY_RUN" = true ]; then
    emit "[DRY-RUN] $*"
  else
    emit "$*"
  fi
}

log_verbose() {
  [ "$VERBOSE" = true ] || return 0
  emit "$*"
}

etime_to_seconds() {
  local raw="$1"
  local days=0
  local rest="$raw"
  local h=0
  local m=0
  local s=0
  local a b c

  case "$rest" in
    *-*)
      days="${rest%%-*}"
      rest="${rest#*-}"
      ;;
  esac

  IFS=: read -r a b c <<EOF
$rest
EOF
  if [ -n "${c:-}" ]; then
    h="$a"
    m="$b"
    s="$c"
  elif [ -n "${b:-}" ]; then
    m="$a"
    s="$b"
  else
    s="$a"
  fi

  days="${days#0}"; h="${h#0}"; m="${m#0}"; s="${s#0}"
  days="${days:-0}"; h="${h:-0}"; m="${m:-0}"; s="${s:-0}"
  printf '%s\n' $((days * 86400 + h * 3600 + m * 60 + s))
}

classify_command() {
  local cmd="$1"
  case "$cmd" in
    *"mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs"*) printf '%s\n' "mk-spec-memory-launcher"; return 0 ;;
    *"mk-skill-advisor-launcher.cjs"*) printf '%s\n' "mk-skill-advisor-launcher"; return 0 ;;
    *"mk-code-index-launcher.cjs"*) printf '%s\n' "mk-code-index-launcher"; return 0 ;;
    *"system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/context-server.js"*) printf '%s\n' "spec-memory-context-server"; return 0 ;;
    *"system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/dist/"*"advisor-server.js"*) printf '%s\n' "skill-advisor-server"; return 0 ;;
    *"system-code-graph/mcp_server/dist/index.js"*) printf '%s\n' "code-graph-server"; return 0 ;;
    *"mcp-code-mode/mcp_server/dist/index.js"*) printf '%s\n' "mcp-code-mode"; return 0 ;;
    *"npm exec @taazkareem/clickup-mcp-server"*) printf '%s\n' "clickup-mcp-wrapper"; return 0 ;;
    *"clickup-mcp-server"*) printf '%s\n' "clickup-mcp-server"; return 0 ;;
    *"npm exec @modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking"*)
      printf '%s\n' "sequential-thinking-wrapper"
      return 0
      ;;
    *"@modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking"*|*"server-sequential-thinking"*)
      printf '%s\n' "sequential-thinking-server"
      return 0
      ;;
  esac
  return 1
}

class_index() {
  local class="$1"
  local i
  for i in "${!min_classes[@]}"; do
    if [ "${min_classes[$i]}" = "$class" ]; then
      printf '%s\n' "$i"
      return 0
    fi
  done
  return 1
}

remember_min_for_class() {
  local class="$1"
  local age="$2"
  local pid="$3"
  local idx
  idx="$(class_index "$class" 2>/dev/null || true)"
  if [ -z "$idx" ]; then
    min_classes+=("$class")
    min_ages+=("$age")
    min_pids+=("$pid")
    return
  fi
  if [ "$age" -lt "${min_ages[$idx]}" ]; then
    min_ages[$idx]="$age"
    min_pids[$idx]="$pid"
  fi
}

freshest_pid_for_class() {
  local class="$1"
  local idx
  idx="$(class_index "$class" 2>/dev/null || true)"
  [ -n "$idx" ] || return 1
  printf '%s\n' "${min_pids[$idx]}"
}

freshest_age_for_class() {
  local class="$1"
  local idx
  idx="$(class_index "$class" 2>/dev/null || true)"
  [ -n "$idx" ] || return 1
  printf '%s\n' "${min_ages[$idx]}"
}

pid_in_list() {
  local needle="$1"
  shift
  local item
  for item in "$@"; do
    [ "$item" = "$needle" ] && return 0
  done
  return 1
}

append_descendants() {
  local parent="$1"
  local child
  while read -r child; do
    [ -n "$child" ] || continue
    if [ "${#session_tree_pids[@]}" -eq 0 ] || ! pid_in_list "$child" "${session_tree_pids[@]}"; then
      session_tree_pids+=("$child")
      append_descendants "$child"
    fi
  done <<EOF
$(pgrep -P "$parent" 2>/dev/null || true)
EOF
}

# Build the live operator-session process tree across ALL AI CLI runtimes, not just
# Claude Code. Any MCP helper descended from a live claude / opencode / codex
# session belongs to an active operator session and must be preserved — most importantly
# an operator's `opencode run`, which previously fell outside the Claude-only tree and
# got swept after ORPHAN_AGE_MIN_SEC (a hard-rule violation).
build_session_trees() {
  local pid
  while read -r pid; do
    [ -n "$pid" ] || continue
    if [ "${#session_tree_pids[@]}" -eq 0 ] || ! pid_in_list "$pid" "${session_tree_pids[@]}"; then
      session_tree_pids+=("$pid")
      append_descendants "$pid"
    fi
  done <<EOF
$(pgrep -f '(^|/)(claude|opencode|codex)( |$)|Claude Code' 2>/dev/null || true)
EOF
}

has_non_mcp_listener() {
  local pid="$1"
  local line
  while read -r line; do
    case "$line" in
      *LISTEN*)
        return 0
        ;;
    esac
  done <<EOF
$(lsof -nP -a -p "$pid" -iTCP -sTCP:LISTEN 2>/dev/null || true)
EOF
  return 1
}

# The MCP daemons bridge sibling sessions over a UNIX socket (daemon-ipc.sock),
# NOT TCP, so has_non_mcp_listener never covers them. Under default-on daemon
# re-election a still-serving daemon is reparented to pid 1 and falls outside every
# live-session tree — yet killing it drops the transport for any concurrent live
# session bridged to it. A daemon actively serving connections holds MORE THAN ONE
# unix-socket FD on its daemon-ipc.sock (the listener + one per live peer); a bare
# listener with no clients holds exactly one. Treat >1 as "in use" and preserve it.
has_live_ipc_socket_connection() {
  local pid="$1"
  local count
  count="$(lsof -nP -a -p "$pid" -U 2>/dev/null | grep -c 'daemon-ipc\.sock' || true)"
  [ -n "$count" ] || count=0
  [ "$count" -gt 1 ] 2>/dev/null
}

preserve_reason() {
  local pid="$1"
  local class="$2"
  local age="$3"
  local cmd="$4"
  local freshest_pid
  local freshest_age

  # Operator-owned interactive/dispatch sessions across runtimes. These commands name a
  # session the operator is actively running; their MCP helpers must never be swept.
  # build_session_trees covers descendants via process ancestry; these patterns also
  # catch the session's own root process whatever its descendants look like.
  case "$cmd" in
    *"opencode run"*) printf '%s\n' "operator-opencode-preserve"; return 0 ;;
    *"codex exec"*) printf '%s\n' "operator-codex-preserve"; return 0 ;;
    *"ollama runner"*|*"ollama serve"*) printf '%s\n' "ollama-preserve"; return 0 ;;
  esac

  if [ "${#session_tree_pids[@]}" -gt 0 ] && pid_in_list "$pid" "${session_tree_pids[@]}"; then
    printf '%s\n' "live-session-tree"
    return 0
  fi

  freshest_pid="$(freshest_pid_for_class "$class" 2>/dev/null || true)"
  freshest_age="$(freshest_age_for_class "$class" 2>/dev/null || echo 999999)"
  if [ "$pid" = "$freshest_pid" ] && [ "$freshest_age" -lt "$AGE_MIN_SEC" ]; then
    printf '%s\n' "freshest-young-instance"
    return 0
  fi

  if has_non_mcp_listener "$pid"; then
    printf '%s\n' "active-non-mcp-tcp-listener"
    return 0
  fi

  if has_live_ipc_socket_connection "$pid"; then
    printf '%s\n' "active-ipc-socket-connection"
    return 0
  fi

  return 1
}

scan_processes() {
  local pid ppid etime rss stat command class age
  while read -r pid ppid etime rss stat command; do
    [ -n "${pid:-}" ] || continue
    class="$(classify_command "${command:-}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
    [ -n "$class" ] || continue
    age="$(etime_to_seconds "$etime")"
    candidate_pids+=("$pid")
    candidate_ppids+=("$ppid")
    candidate_classes+=("$class")
    candidate_ages+=("$age")
    candidate_etimes+=("$etime")
    candidate_rsses+=("$rss")
    candidate_commands+=("${command:-}")
    remember_min_for_class "$class" "$age" "$pid"
  done <<EOF
$(ps -axo pid=,ppid=,etime=,rss=,stat=,command=)
EOF
}

select_kill_candidates() {
  local i pid class age etime rss cmd reason
  for i in "${!candidate_pids[@]}"; do
    pid="${candidate_pids[$i]}"
    class="${candidate_classes[$i]}"
    age="${candidate_ages[$i]}"
    etime="${candidate_etimes[$i]}"
    rss="${candidate_rsses[$i]}"
    cmd="${candidate_commands[$i]}"

    reason="$(preserve_reason "$pid" "$class" "$age" "$cmd" 2>/dev/null || true)"
    if [ -n "$reason" ]; then
      log_verbose "action=preserve reason=$reason pid=$pid class=$class rss_kb=$rss age_sec=$age etime=$etime command=$cmd"
      continue
    fi

    kill_pids+=("$pid")
    kill_classes+=("$class")
    kill_ages+=("$age")
    kill_etimes+=("$etime")
    kill_rsses+=("$rss")
    kill_commands+=("$cmd")
  done
}

terminate_candidates() {
  local i pid class age etime rss cmd
  for i in "${!kill_pids[@]}"; do
    pid="${kill_pids[$i]}"
    class="${kill_classes[$i]}"
    age="${kill_ages[$i]}"
    etime="${kill_etimes[$i]}"
    rss="${kill_rsses[$i]}"
    cmd="${kill_commands[$i]}"

    log_action "action=kill signal=TERM pid=$pid class=$class rss_kb=$rss age_sec=$age etime=$etime command=$cmd"
    if [ "$DRY_RUN" = false ]; then
      if ! kill -15 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
        emit "action=kill-failed signal=TERM pid=$pid class=$class"
        EXIT_CODE=1
      fi
    fi
  done

  [ "$DRY_RUN" = true ] && return 0
  [ "${#kill_pids[@]}" -eq 0 ] && return 0

  sleep 5
  for i in "${!kill_pids[@]}"; do
    pid="${kill_pids[$i]}"
    class="${kill_classes[$i]}"
    if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
      emit "action=kill signal=KILL pid=$pid class=$class"
      if ! kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
        emit "action=kill-failed signal=KILL pid=$pid class=$class"
        EXIT_CODE=1
      fi
    fi
  done
}

path_mtime_epoch() {
  local path="$1"
  stat -f %m "$path" 2>/dev/null || stat -c %Y "$path" 2>/dev/null || printf '%s\n' 0
}

path_is_old_enough() {
  local path="$1"
  local now_epoch mtime age min_age
  now_epoch="$(date +%s)"
  mtime="$(path_mtime_epoch "$path")"
  is_positive_int "$mtime" || return 1
  age=$((now_epoch - mtime))
  min_age=$((TMP_AGE_HOURS * 3600))
  [ "$age" -ge "$min_age" ]
}

preserve_tmp_path() {
  local path="$1"
  local base="${path##*/}"
  case "$base" in
    codex-browser-use|*cache*|*Cache*) return 0 ;;
  esac
  return 1
}

remove_tmp_path() {
  local path="$1"
  [ -e "$path" ] || return 0
  preserve_tmp_path "$path" && { log_verbose "action=preserve-tmp path=$path"; return 0; }
  path_is_old_enough "$path" || { log_verbose "action=preserve-tmp reason=too-new path=$path"; return 0; }

  log_action "action=remove-tmp path=$path"
  if [ "$DRY_RUN" = false ]; then
    if ! rm -rf "$path" 2>/dev/null; then
      emit "action=remove-tmp-failed path=$path"
      EXIT_CODE=1
    fi
  fi
}

sweep_tmp() {
  local path
  shopt -s nullglob
  for path in /tmp/codex-* /tmp/cli-* /tmp/opencode-* /tmp/deep-review-*; do
    [ -d "$path" ] || continue
    remove_tmp_path "$path"
  done
  for path in /tmp/save-context-data-* /tmp/*-prompt.md /tmp/*-cli-err.log /tmp/*-cli-out.log; do
    [ -f "$path" ] || continue
    remove_tmp_path "$path"
  done
  shopt -u nullglob
}

# Sourcing guard: when this file is sourced (not executed) the caller only wants the
# function definitions (e.g. a unit test exercising has_live_ipc_socket_connection),
# so skip the process-scanning/kill main flow.
(return 0 2>/dev/null) && return 0

rotate_log_if_needed
emit "action=start dry_run=$DRY_RUN verbose=$VERBOSE age_min_sec=$AGE_MIN_SEC tmp_age_hours=$TMP_AGE_HOURS"
build_session_trees
scan_processes
select_kill_candidates
terminate_candidates
sweep_tmp
emit "action=summary candidates=${#candidate_pids[@]} kills=${#kill_pids[@]} exit_code=$EXIT_CODE"
exit "$EXIT_CODE"

#
# Hooks installed:
#   pre-commit  — runs validate-doc-model-refs.js (advisory, non-blocking)
#
# Bypass any installed hook: SPECKIT_SKIP_DOC_MODEL_VALIDATE=1 git commit ...

set -euo pipefail

display.text:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Install advisory git hooks shipped under .opencode/scripts/git-hooks/.
#
# Usage:
#   bash .opencode/scripts/install-git-hooks.sh             # install
#   bash .opencode/scripts/install-git-hooks.sh --uninstall # remove
#
# Hooks installed:
#   pre-commit  — runs validate-doc-model-refs.js (advisory, non-blocking)
#
# Bypass any installed hook: SPECKIT_SKIP_DOC_MODEL_VALIDATE=1 git commit ...

set -euo pipefail

REPO_ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null)"
if [ -z "$REPO_ROOT" ]; then
  echo "ERROR: not inside a git working tree" >&2
  exit 1
fi

HOOK_SOURCE_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/.opencode/scripts/git-hooks"
HOOK_TARGET_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/.git/hooks"

if [ "${1:-}" = "--uninstall" ]; then
  for hook in "$HOOK_SOURCE_DIR"/*; do
    [ -f "$hook" ] || continue
    name="$(basename "$hook")"
    target="$HOOK_TARGET_DIR/$name"
    if [ -L "$target" ]; then
      rm -f "$target"
      echo "removed symlink: $target"
    elif [ -f "$target" ]; then
      echo "skipped (not a symlink we installed): $target" >&2
    fi
  done
  exit 0
fi

mkdir -p "$HOOK_TARGET_DIR"
for hook in "$HOOK_SOURCE_DIR"/*; do
  [ -f "$hook" ] || continue
  name="$(basename "$hook")"
  target="$HOOK_TARGET_DIR/$name"
  if [ -e "$target" ] && [ ! -L "$target" ]; then
    echo "WARNING: $target exists and is not a symlink — skipping" >&2
    continue
  fi
  chmod +x "$hook"
  ln -sf "$hook" "$target"
  echo "installed: $target -> $hook"
done

echo ""
echo "Hooks installed. Test: 'git commit --allow-empty -m \"hook smoke\"' should run the validator silently (no output unless drift)."
echo "Bypass: SPECKIT_SKIP_DOC_MODEL_VALIDATE=1 git commit ..."

display.text:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Copy non-TS assets into the skill-advisor compiled dist tree.
#
# tsc doesn't copy non-TS files (e.g., data/prompt-policy.default.json).
# Without this step, the MCP launcher fails on first start with:
#   ENOENT: no such file or directory, open
#     dist/mcp_server/data/prompt-policy.default.json
#
# Usage:
#   bash .opencode/scripts/copy-skill-advisor-dist-data.sh
#
# `npm run build` runs this copy as its postbuild step. Keep this script as
# a manual repair helper when dist data is missing or stale.
#
# Idempotent. Safe to re-run. Exits 0 if source files are missing (no-op).

set -euo pipefail

REPO_ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null)"
if [ -z "$REPO_ROOT" ]; then
  echo "ERROR: not inside a git working tree" >&2
  exit 1
fi

SRC="$REPO_ROOT/.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/data"
DST="$REPO_ROOT/.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/dist/mcp_server/data"

if [ ! -d "$SRC" ]; then
  echo "skipped: source dir not present ($SRC)" >&2
  exit 0
fi

mkdir -p "$DST"
copied=0
shopt -s nullglob
for f in "$SRC"/*.json; do
  cp "$f" "$DST/"
  copied=$((copied + 1))
done

if [ "$copied" -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "no JSON files to copy in $SRC" >&2
else
  echo "copied $copied JSON file(s) to $DST"
fi

1: #!/usr/bin/env bash
2: # Clean up MCP helper descendants owned by the current Claude Code session.
3: #
4: # This script is intentionally session-scoped: it only ever kills processes it
5: # can prove are descendants of CLAUDE_SESSION_PID, and it re-proves that
6: # ancestry immediately before each kill. There is deliberately NO fallback to
7: # the hook's PPID — under a shared terminal that PPID can resolve to an
8: # ancestor common to MANY live sessions, turning "this session's descendants"
9: # into "every session's MCP helpers" and killing sibling sessions' transports
10: # mid-flight. Missing session identity means we do nothing.
11: 
12: set -euo pipefail
13: 
14: SESSION_PID="${CLAUDE_SESSION_PID:-}"
15: LOG_PATH="${CLAUDE_SESSION_CLEANUP_LOG_PATH:-${HOME:-/tmp}/.local/share/claude-stop-hook.log}"
16: EXIT_CODE=0
17: descendants=()
18: 
19: # Orphan-sweep fallback for the no-session-pid case. Default off. "dry-run" logs candidate reaps
20: # without mutating; "1"/"on"/"live" reaps. It delegates to the ORPHAN-ONLY sweeper, which reaps only
21: # ownerless (reparented) MCP processes and so can never touch a live sibling session — unlike a PPID
22: # guess, which this script deliberately refuses (see header). The sweeper path is overridable for tests.
23: SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
24: ORPHAN_SWEEP_MODE="${SPECKIT_STOP_HOOK_ORPHAN_SWEEP:-off}"
25: ORPHAN_SWEEPER_BIN="${SPECKIT_ORPHAN_SWEEPER_BIN:-$SCRIPT_DIR/orphan-mcp-sweeper.sh}"
26: 
27: emit() {
28:   local line
29:   line="$(date '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z') $*"
30:   printf '%s\n' "$line"
31:   if [ -n "$LOG_PATH" ]; then
32:     mkdir -p "$(dirname "$LOG_PATH")" 2>/dev/null || true
33:     printf '%s\n' "$line" >> "$LOG_PATH" 2>/dev/null || true
34:   fi
35: }
36: 
37: pid_in_descendants() {
38:   local needle="$1"
39:   local item
40:   [ "${#descendants[@]}" -gt 0 ] || return 1
41:   for item in "${descendants[@]}"; do
42:     [ "$item" = "$needle" ] && return 0
43:   done
44:   return 1
45: }
46: 
47: append_descendants() {
48:   local parent="$1"
49:   local child
50:   while read -r child; do
51:     [ -n "$child" ] || continue
52:     if ! pid_in_descendants "$child"; then
53:       descendants+=("$child")
54:       append_descendants "$child"
55:     fi
56:   done <<EOF
57: $(pgrep -P "$parent" 2>/dev/null || true)
58: EOF
59: }
60: 
61: is_target_command() {
62:   local cmd="$1"
63:   case "$cmd" in
64:     *"mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs"*|\
65:     *"mk-skill-advisor-launcher.cjs"*|\
66:     *"mk-code-index-launcher.cjs"*|\
67:     *"system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/context-server.js"*|\
68:     *"system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/dist/"*"advisor-server.js"*|\
69:     *"system-code-graph/mcp_server/dist/index.js"*|\
70:     *"mcp-code-mode/mcp_server/dist/index.js"*|\
71:     *"@modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking"*|\
72:     *"server-sequential-thinking"*|\
73:     *"clickup-mcp-server"*)
74:       return 0
75:       ;;
76:   esac
77:   return 1
78: }
79: 
80: # Re-prove, at kill time, that a candidate is still a descendant of the
81: # session. The pgrep walk above is a snapshot; a process can be re-parented
82: # (orphan adoption by launchd/init) between the walk and the kill, and a pid
83: # can in principle be recycled. Walking the live ppid chain upward closes
84: # that race: a process belonging to a sibling session can never have OUR
85: # session pid in its ancestor chain.
86: is_descendant_of_session() {
87:   local pid="$1"
88:   local depth=0
89:   local current="$pid"
90:   while [ "$depth" -lt 15 ]; do
91:     current="$(ps -o ppid= -p "$current" 2>/dev/null | tr -d ' ' || true)"
92:     [ -n "$current" ] || return 1
93:     [ "$current" = "0" ] && return 1
94:     [ "$current" = "1" ] && return 1
95:     if [ "$current" = "$SESSION_PID" ]; then
96:       return 0
97:     fi
98:     depth=$((depth + 1))
99:   done
100:   return 1
101: }
102: 
103: # Without a session pid the session-scoped kill below is impossible, and guessing is unsafe. Fall
104: # back to the orphan-only sweeper when explicitly enabled; otherwise keep the historical no-op.
105: run_orphan_sweep_fallback() {
106:   case "$ORPHAN_SWEEP_MODE" in
107:     1|on|live)
108:       emit "action=orphan-sweep mode=live reason=no-session-pid"
109:       bash "$ORPHAN_SWEEPER_BIN" || true
110:       ;;
111:     dry-run|dryrun|dry)
112:       emit "action=orphan-sweep mode=dry-run reason=no-session-pid"
113:       bash "$ORPHAN_SWEEPER_BIN" --dry-run || true
114:       ;;
115:     *)
116:       emit "action=skip reason=no-session-pid"
117:       ;;
118:   esac
119: }
120: 
121: if [ -z "$SESSION_PID" ]; then
122:   run_orphan_sweep_fallback
123:   exit 0
124: fi
125: if ! kill -0 "$SESSION_PID" 2>/dev/null; then
126:   emit "action=skip reason=session-pid-not-alive session_pid=$SESSION_PID"
127:   exit 0
128: fi
129: 
130: append_descendants "$SESSION_PID"
131: emit "action=start session_pid=$SESSION_PID descendants=${#descendants[@]}"
132: 
133: if [ "${#descendants[@]}" -gt 0 ]; then
134:   for pid in "${descendants[@]}"; do
135:     cmd="$(ps -p "$pid" -o command= 2>/dev/null || true)"
136:     [ -n "$cmd" ] || continue
137:     if is_target_command "$cmd"; then
138:       if ! is_descendant_of_session "$pid"; then
139:         emit "action=skip-kill reason=ancestry-not-confirmed pid=$pid matched_by=name ancestor_ok=no command=$cmd"
140:         continue
141:       fi
142:       emit "action=kill signal=TERM pid=$pid matched_by=name ancestor_ok=yes command=$cmd"
143:       if ! kill -15 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
144:         emit "action=kill-failed signal=TERM pid=$pid"
145:         EXIT_CODE=1
146:       fi
147:     fi
148:   done
149: fi
150: 
151: emit "action=summary session_pid=$SESSION_PID exit_code=$EXIT_CODE"
152: exit "$EXIT_CODE"

# Orphan-sweep fallback for the no-session-pid case. Default off. "dry-run" logs candidate reaps

display.text:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Clean up MCP helper descendants owned by the current Claude Code session.
#
# This script is intentionally session-scoped: it only ever kills processes it
# can prove are descendants of CLAUDE_SESSION_PID, and it re-proves that
# ancestry immediately before each kill. There is deliberately NO fallback to
# the hook's PPID — under a shared terminal that PPID can resolve to an
# ancestor common to MANY live sessions, turning "this session's descendants"
# into "every session's MCP helpers" and killing sibling sessions' transports
# mid-flight. Missing session identity means we do nothing.

set -euo pipefail

SESSION_PID="${CLAUDE_SESSION_PID:-}"
LOG_PATH="${CLAUDE_SESSION_CLEANUP_LOG_PATH:-${HOME:-/tmp}/.local/share/claude-stop-hook.log}"
EXIT_CODE=0
descendants=()

# Orphan-sweep fallback for the no-session-pid case. Default off. "dry-run" logs candidate reaps
# without mutating; "1"/"on"/"live" reaps. It delegates to the ORPHAN-ONLY sweeper, which reaps only
# ownerless (reparented) MCP processes and so can never touch a live sibling session — unlike a PPID
# guess, which this script deliberately refuses (see header). The sweeper path is overridable for tests.
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
ORPHAN_SWEEP_MODE="${SPECKIT_STOP_HOOK_ORPHAN_SWEEP:-off}"
ORPHAN_SWEEPER_BIN="${SPECKIT_ORPHAN_SWEEPER_BIN:-$SCRIPT_DIR/orphan-mcp-sweeper.sh}"

emit() {
  local line
  line="$(date '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z') $*"
  printf '%s\n' "$line"
  if [ -n "$LOG_PATH" ]; then
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$LOG_PATH")" 2>/dev/null || true
    printf '%s\n' "$line" >> "$LOG_PATH" 2>/dev/null || true
  fi
}

pid_in_descendants() {
  local needle="$1"
  local item
  [ "${#descendants[@]}" -gt 0 ] || return 1
  for item in "${descendants[@]}"; do
    [ "$item" = "$needle" ] && return 0
  done
  return 1
}

append_descendants() {
  local parent="$1"
  local child
  while read -r child; do
    [ -n "$child" ] || continue
    if ! pid_in_descendants "$child"; then
      descendants+=("$child")
      append_descendants "$child"
    fi
  done <<EOF
$(pgrep -P "$parent" 2>/dev/null || true)
EOF
}

is_target_command() {
  local cmd="$1"
  case "$cmd" in
    *"mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs"*|\
    *"mk-skill-advisor-launcher.cjs"*|\
    *"mk-code-index-launcher.cjs"*|\
    *"system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/context-server.js"*|\
    *"system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/dist/"*"advisor-server.js"*|\
    *"system-code-graph/mcp_server/dist/index.js"*|\
    *"mcp-code-mode/mcp_server/dist/index.js"*|\
    *"@modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking"*|\
    *"server-sequential-thinking"*|\
    *"clickup-mcp-server"*)
      return 0
      ;;
  esac
  return 1
}

# Re-prove, at kill time, that a candidate is still a descendant of the
# session. The pgrep walk above is a snapshot; a process can be re-parented
# (orphan adoption by launchd/init) between the walk and the kill, and a pid
# can in principle be recycled. Walking the live ppid chain upward closes
# that race: a process belonging to a sibling session can never have OUR
# session pid in its ancestor chain.
is_descendant_of_session() {
  local pid="$1"
  local depth=0
  local current="$pid"
  while [ "$depth" -lt 15 ]; do
    current="$(ps -o ppid= -p "$current" 2>/dev/null | tr -d ' ' || true)"
    [ -n "$current" ] || return 1
    [ "$current" = "0" ] && return 1
    [ "$current" = "1" ] && return 1
    if [ "$current" = "$SESSION_PID" ]; then
      return 0
    fi
    depth=$((depth + 1))
  done
  return 1
}

# Without a session pid the session-scoped kill below is impossible, and guessing is unsafe. Fall
# back to the orphan-only sweeper when explicitly enabled; otherwise keep the historical no-op.
run_orphan_sweep_fallback() {
  case "$ORPHAN_SWEEP_MODE" in
    1|on|live)
      emit "action=orphan-sweep mode=live reason=no-session-pid"
      bash "$ORPHAN_SWEEPER_BIN" || true
      ;;
    dry-run|dryrun|dry)
      emit "action=orphan-sweep mode=dry-run reason=no-session-pid"
      bash "$ORPHAN_SWEEPER_BIN" --dry-run || true
      ;;
    *)
      emit "action=skip reason=no-session-pid"
      ;;
  esac
}

if [ -z "$SESSION_PID" ]; then
  run_orphan_sweep_fallback
  exit 0
fi
if ! kill -0 "$SESSION_PID" 2>/dev/null; then
  emit "action=skip reason=session-pid-not-alive session_pid=$SESSION_PID"
  exit 0
fi

append_descendants "$SESSION_PID"
emit "action=start session_pid=$SESSION_PID descendants=${#descendants[@]}"

if [ "${#descendants[@]}" -gt 0 ]; then
  for pid in "${descendants[@]}"; do
    cmd="$(ps -p "$pid" -o command= 2>/dev/null || true)"
    [ -n "$cmd" ] || continue
    if is_target_command "$cmd"; then
      if ! is_descendant_of_session "$pid"; then
        emit "action=skip-kill reason=ancestry-not-confirmed pid=$pid matched_by=name ancestor_ok=no command=$cmd"
        continue
      fi
      emit "action=kill signal=TERM pid=$pid matched_by=name ancestor_ok=yes command=$cmd"
      if ! kill -15 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
        emit "action=kill-failed signal=TERM pid=$pid"
        EXIT_CODE=1
      fi
    fi
  done
fi

emit "action=summary session_pid=$SESSION_PID exit_code=$EXIT_CODE"
exit "$EXIT_CODE"

1: #!/usr/bin/env bash
2: # Post-commit hook — advisory code-graph staleness mitigation.
3: #
4: # When a commit changes more files than the configured threshold, mark the
5: # code-graph SQLite stale so the next MCP launcher boot triggers a fresh
6: # full scan. Without this, drift accumulates silently (the launcher only
7: # detects drift at boot; multiple commits without a launcher restart can
8: # leave the graph weeks out of date). Runtime-agnostic: the launcher boots
9: # under any AI CLI runtime (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex), so this
10: # applies to whichever runtime starts the next session.
11: #
12: # Default threshold: 100 files changed (post-commit). Tunable via
13: #   SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_POST_COMMIT_REBUILD_THRESHOLD=<int>
14: # Bypass entirely (skip hook): SPECKIT_SKIP_CODE_GRAPH_POST_COMMIT=1
15: # Disable just the rebuild (still print advisory): SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_POST_COMMIT_DRY_RUN=1
16: #
17: # Install: bash .opencode/scripts/install-git-hooks.sh
18: # Uninstall: bash .opencode/scripts/install-git-hooks.sh --uninstall
19: 
20: set -u
21: 
22: if [ "${SPECKIT_SKIP_CODE_GRAPH_POST_COMMIT:-0}" = "1" ]; then
23:   exit 0
24: fi
25: 
26: REPO_ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null)"
27: if [ -z "$REPO_ROOT" ]; then
28:   exit 0
29: fi

metadata.preview:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Post-commit hook — advisory code-graph staleness mitigation.
#
# When a commit changes more files than the configured threshold, mark the
# code-graph SQLite stale so the next MCP launcher boot triggers a fresh
# full scan. Without this, drift accumulates silently (the launcher only
# detects drift at boot; multiple commits without a launcher restart can
# leave the graph weeks out of date). Runtime-agnostic: the launcher boots
# under any AI CLI runtime (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex), so this
# applies to whichever runtime starts the next session.
#
# Default threshold: 100 files changed (post-commit). Tunable via
#   SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_POST_COMMIT_REBUILD_THRESHOLD=<int>
# Bypass entirely (skip hook): SPECKIT_SKIP_CODE_GRAPH_POST_COMMIT=1
# Disable just the rebuild (still print advisory): SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_POST_COMMIT_DRY_RUN=1
#
# Install: bash .opencode/scripts/install-git-hooks.sh
# Uninstall: bash .opencode/scripts/install-git-hooks.sh --uninstall

set -u

display.text:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Post-commit hook — advisory code-graph staleness mitigation.
#
# When a commit changes more files than the configured threshold, mark the
# code-graph SQLite stale so the next MCP launcher boot triggers a fresh
# full scan. Without this, drift accumulates silently (the launcher only
# detects drift at boot; multiple commits without a launcher restart can
# leave the graph weeks out of date). Runtime-agnostic: the launcher boots
# under any AI CLI runtime (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex), so this
# applies to whichever runtime starts the next session.
#
# Default threshold: 100 files changed (post-commit). Tunable via
#   SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_POST_COMMIT_REBUILD_THRESHOLD=<int>
# Bypass entirely (skip hook): SPECKIT_SKIP_CODE_GRAPH_POST_COMMIT=1
# Disable just the rebuild (still print advisory): SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_POST_COMMIT_DRY_RUN=1
#
# Install: bash .opencode/scripts/install-git-hooks.sh
# Uninstall: bash .opencode/scripts/install-git-hooks.sh --uninstall

set -u

if [ "${SPECKIT_SKIP_CODE_GRAPH_POST_COMMIT:-0}" = "1" ]; then
  exit 0
fi

REPO_ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null)"
if [ -z "$REPO_ROOT" ]; then
  exit 0
fi

THRESHOLD="${SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_POST_COMMIT_REBUILD_THRESHOLD:-100}"
DRY_RUN="${SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_POST_COMMIT_DRY_RUN:-0}"

# Count files changed in the just-completed commit (HEAD).
CHANGED="$(git diff-tree --no-commit-id --name-only -r HEAD 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')"
if [ -z "$CHANGED" ] || [ "$CHANGED" -lt 1 ]; then
  exit 0
fi

if [ "$CHANGED" -lt "$THRESHOLD" ]; then
  # Small commit — let the launcher's selective-rescan path handle it on next boot.
  exit 0
fi

DB_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/.opencode/.spec-kit/code-graph/database"
SQLITE="$DB_DIR/code-graph.sqlite"
SHM="$DB_DIR/code-graph.sqlite-shm"
WAL="$DB_DIR/code-graph.sqlite-wal"
READINESS="$DB_DIR/.code-graph-readiness.json"

if [ ! -f "$SQLITE" ] && [ ! -f "$READINESS" ]; then
  # Nothing to invalidate — already absent or never built.
  exit 0
fi

if [ "$DRY_RUN" = "1" ]; then
  echo "" >&2
  echo "⚠️  code-graph: $CHANGED files changed in HEAD (>= $THRESHOLD); DRY-RUN — would invalidate $DB_DIR" >&2
  echo "" >&2
  exit 0
fi

# Invalidate the DB + lease files. Next launcher boot will detect empty DB
# and run an inline full scan during MCP startup.
rm -f "$SQLITE" "$SHM" "$WAL" "$READINESS" \
      "$DB_DIR/.mk-code-index-launcher.json" \
      "$DB_DIR/.code-graph-owner.json"

echo "" >&2
echo "⚠️  code-graph: $CHANGED files changed in HEAD (>= $THRESHOLD)" >&2
echo "    Invalidated code-graph SQLite + lease files." >&2
echo "    Next Claude Code session will run an inline full scan on boot (~3-5 min)." >&2
echo "    Bypass next time: SPECKIT_SKIP_CODE_GRAPH_POST_COMMIT=1 git commit ..." >&2
echo "    Adjust threshold: SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_POST_COMMIT_REBUILD_THRESHOLD=<int>" >&2
echo "" >&2

exit 0

1: #!/usr/bin/env bash
2: # Pre-commit hook — advisory model-reference drift check.
3: #
4: # Runs validate-doc-model-refs.js on the working tree before each commit.
5: # Advisory mode: prints warnings on drift but does NOT block the commit.
6: # Bypass: SPECKIT_SKIP_DOC_MODEL_VALIDATE=1 git commit ...
7: #
8: # Install: bash .opencode/scripts/install-git-hooks.sh
9: # Uninstall: rm .git/hooks/pre-commit
10: 
11: set -u
12: 
13: if [ "${SPECKIT_SKIP_DOC_MODEL_VALIDATE:-0}" = "1" ]; then
14:   exit 0
15: fi
16: 
17: REPO_ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null)"
18: if [ -z "$REPO_ROOT" ]; then
19:   exit 0
20: fi
21: 
22: VALIDATOR="$REPO_ROOT/.opencode/skills/sk-doc/scripts/validate-doc-model-refs.js"
23: if [ ! -f "$VALIDATOR" ]; then
24:   exit 0
25: fi
26: 
27: if ! command -v node >/dev/null 2>&1; then
28:   exit 0
29: fi
30: 
31: # Run validator; capture exit code. Non-zero exit means drift detected.
32: # Advisory: warn on stderr but always exit 0 so the commit proceeds.
33: OUT="$(node "$VALIDATOR" 2>&1)"
34: RC=$?
35: 
36: if [ "$RC" -ne 0 ]; then
37:   echo "" >&2
38:   echo "⚠️  doc-model-refs drift detected (advisory; commit not blocked):" >&2
39:   echo "$OUT" | head -30 >&2
40:   echo "" >&2
41:   echo "Bypass next time: SPECKIT_SKIP_DOC_MODEL_VALIDATE=1 git commit ..." >&2
42:   echo "Fix: update docs to cite canonical model names from registry.ts" >&2
43:   echo "" >&2
44: fi
45: 
46: # --- Comment hygiene gate (blocking) ---
47: # Blocks commits that add ephemeral-artifact pointers in code comment lines.
48: # Bypass: SPECKIT_SKIP_COMMENT_HYGIENE=1 git commit ...
49: # See: .opencode/skills/sk-code/references/universal/code_style_guide.md §4
50: if [ "${SPECKIT_SKIP_COMMENT_HYGIENE:-0}" != "1" ]; then
51:   HYGIENE_HOOK="${REPO_ROOT}/.opencode/hooks/pre-commit"
52:   if [ -x "$HYGIENE_HOOK" ]; then
53:     bash "$HYGIENE_HOOK" || exit $?
54:   fi
55: fi
56: 
57: # --- Prompt-knowledge card-sync gate (blocking) ---
58: # When a prompt-knowledge surface is staged, run the 3-layer drift guard so
59: # table inlining, Tier-3 escalation-rule drift, registry/profile/_index gaps
60: # (zero-hub-weight entries), or models unreachable by name cannot silently
61: # regress across sk-prompt / sk-prompt-models / cli-*.
62: # Bypass: SPECKIT_SKIP_CARD_SYNC=1 git commit ...
63: if [ "${SPECKIT_SKIP_CARD_SYNC:-0}" != "1" ]; then
64:   CARD_GUARD="${REPO_ROOT}/.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/scripts/check-prompt-quality-card-sync.sh"
65:   if [ -f "$CARD_GUARD" ] && git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACM \
66:        | grep -Eq '^\.opencode/skills/(cli-[a-z-]+/(SKILL\.md|assets/prompt_quality_card\.md|graph-metadata\.json)|sk-prompt/assets/cli_prompt_quality_card\.md|sk-prompt-models/)'; then
67:     if ! bash "$CARD_GUARD" "$REPO_ROOT"; then
68:       echo "" >&2
69:       echo "BLOCKED: prompt-knowledge drift guard failed (see FAIL lines above)." >&2
70:       echo "Fix: keep each fact in its one canonical home and point to it; see" >&2
71:       echo "     .opencode/skills/sk-prompt-models/references/pattern_index.md." >&2
72:       echo "Bypass: SPECKIT_SKIP_CARD_SYNC=1 git commit ..." >&2
73:       exit 1
74:     fi
75:   fi
76: fi
77: 
78: # --- MCP mutation-class gate (blocking) ---
79: # When an mcp-* install/doctor script or the install-doctor manifest is staged,
80: # enforce the read-only / mutating contract: a "read-only" doctor that grows an
81: # unguarded mutation or unbounded network call (or a missing manifest label)
82: # fails the commit. Guards against the doctor-that-connected-to-all-servers and
83: # the installer-that-wiped-a-global-cache regressions.
84: # Bypass: SPECKIT_SKIP_MCP_MUTATION_CLASS=1 git commit ...
85: if [ "${SPECKIT_SKIP_MCP_MUTATION_CLASS:-0}" != "1" ]; then
86:   MUTCLASS_GUARD="${REPO_ROOT}/.opencode/commands/doctor/scripts/check-mcp-mutation-class.sh"
87:   if [ -f "$MUTCLASS_GUARD" ] && git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACM \
88:        | grep -Eq '^\.opencode/(skills/mcp-[a-z-]+/(scripts/(doctor|install)[a-z.-]*\.sh|mcp-servers/[^/]+/setup\.sh)|commands/doctor/assets/doctor_mcp_install\.yaml)$'; then
89:     if ! bash "$MUTCLASS_GUARD" "$REPO_ROOT"; then
90:       echo "" >&2
91:       echo "BLOCKED: MCP mutation-class contract failed (see FAIL lines above)." >&2
92:       echo "Fix: a read-only doctor must not run mutations or unbounded network calls;" >&2
93:       echo "     declare each script's mutation_class in doctor_mcp_install.yaml." >&2
94:       echo "Bypass: SPECKIT_SKIP_MCP_MUTATION_CLASS=1 git commit ..." >&2
95:       exit 1
96:     fi
97:   fi
98: fi
99: 
100: # --- Tool ownership map gate (blocking) ---
101: # Blocks commits when the committed generated map no longer matches TOOL_DEFINITIONS.
102: # Bypass: SPECKIT_SKIP_TOOL_OWNERSHIP_LINT=1 git commit ...
103: if [ "${SPECKIT_SKIP_TOOL_OWNERSHIP_LINT:-0}" != "1" ]; then
104:   OWNERSHIP_LINT="${REPO_ROOT}/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/tool-ownership-lint-runner.mjs"
105:   if [ ! -f "$OWNERSHIP_LINT" ]; then
106:     echo "BLOCKED: tool-ownership lint runner missing." >&2
107:     exit 1
108:   fi
109:   if ! node "$OWNERSHIP_LINT" >/dev/null; then
110:     echo "" >&2
111:     echo "BLOCKED: tool-ownership drift detected." >&2
112:     echo "Fix: regenerate mcp_server/tests/fixtures/tool-ownership-map.json from TOOL_DEFINITIONS." >&2
113:     echo "Bypass: SPECKIT_SKIP_TOOL_OWNERSHIP_LINT=1 git commit ..." >&2
114:     exit 1
115:   fi
116: fi
117: 
118: exit 0

(End of file - total 118 lines)
</content>

# Runs validate-doc-model-refs.js on the working tree before each commit.
# Advisory mode: prints warnings on drift but does NOT block the commit.
# Bypass: SPECKIT_SKIP_DOC_MODEL_VALIDATE=1 git commit ...
#
# Install: bash .opencode/scripts/install-git-hooks.sh
# Uninstall: rm .git/hooks/pre-commit

set -u

if [ "${SPECKIT_SKIP_DOC_MODEL_VALIDATE:-0}" = "1" ]; then
  exit 0

  exit 0

display.text:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Pre-commit hook — advisory model-reference drift check.
#
# Runs validate-doc-model-refs.js on the working tree before each commit.
# Advisory mode: prints warnings on drift but does NOT block the commit.
# Bypass: SPECKIT_SKIP_DOC_MODEL_VALIDATE=1 git commit ...
#
# Install: bash .opencode/scripts/install-git-hooks.sh
# Uninstall: rm .git/hooks/pre-commit

set -u

if [ "${SPECKIT_SKIP_DOC_MODEL_VALIDATE:-0}" = "1" ]; then
  exit 0
fi

REPO_ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null)"
if [ -z "$REPO_ROOT" ]; then
  exit 0
fi

VALIDATOR="$REPO_ROOT/.opencode/skills/sk-doc/scripts/validate-doc-model-refs.js"
if [ ! -f "$VALIDATOR" ]; then
  exit 0
fi

if ! command -v node >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  exit 0
fi

# Run validator; capture exit code. Non-zero exit means drift detected.
# Advisory: warn on stderr but always exit 0 so the commit proceeds.
OUT="$(node "$VALIDATOR" 2>&1)"
RC=$?

if [ "$RC" -ne 0 ]; then
  echo "" >&2
  echo "⚠️  doc-model-refs drift detected (advisory; commit not blocked):" >&2
  echo "$OUT" | head -30 >&2
  echo "" >&2
  echo "Bypass next time: SPECKIT_SKIP_DOC_MODEL_VALIDATE=1 git commit ..." >&2
  echo "Fix: update docs to cite canonical model names from registry.ts" >&2
  echo "" >&2
fi

# --- Comment hygiene gate (blocking) ---
# Blocks commits that add ephemeral-artifact pointers in code comment lines.
# Bypass: SPECKIT_SKIP_COMMENT_HYGIENE=1 git commit ...
# See: .opencode/skills/sk-code/references/universal/code_style_guide.md §4
if [ "${SPECKIT_SKIP_COMMENT_HYGIENE:-0}" != "1" ]; then
  HYGIENE_HOOK="${REPO_ROOT}/.opencode/hooks/pre-commit"
  if [ -x "$HYGIENE_HOOK" ]; then
    bash "$HYGIENE_HOOK" || exit $?
  fi
fi

# --- Prompt-knowledge card-sync gate (blocking) ---
# When a prompt-knowledge surface is staged, run the 3-layer drift guard so
# table inlining, Tier-3 escalation-rule drift, registry/profile/_index gaps
# (zero-hub-weight entries), or models unreachable by name cannot silently
# regress across sk-prompt / sk-prompt-models / cli-*.
# Bypass: SPECKIT_SKIP_CARD_SYNC=1 git commit ...
if [ "${SPECKIT_SKIP_CARD_SYNC:-0}" != "1" ]; then
  CARD_GUARD="${REPO_ROOT}/.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/scripts/check-prompt-quality-card-sync.sh"
  if [ -f "$CARD_GUARD" ] && git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACM \
       | grep -Eq '^\.opencode/skills/(cli-[a-z-]+/(SKILL\.md|assets/prompt_quality_card\.md|graph-metadata\.json)|sk-prompt/assets/cli_prompt_quality_card\.md|sk-prompt-models/)'; then
    if ! bash "$CARD_GUARD" "$REPO_ROOT"; then
      echo "" >&2
      echo "BLOCKED: prompt-knowledge drift guard failed (see FAIL lines above)." >&2
      echo "Fix: keep each fact in its one canonical home and point to it; see" >&2
      echo "     .opencode/skills/sk-prompt-models/references/pattern_index.md." >&2
      echo "Bypass: SPECKIT_SKIP_CARD_SYNC=1 git commit ..." >&2
      exit 1
    fi
  fi
fi

# --- MCP mutation-class gate (blocking) ---
# When an mcp-* install/doctor script or the install-doctor manifest is staged,
# enforce the read-only / mutating contract: a "read-only" doctor that grows an
# unguarded mutation or unbounded network call (or a missing manifest label)
# fails the commit. Guards against the doctor-that-connected-to-all-servers and
# the installer-that-wiped-a-global-cache regressions.
# Bypass: SPECKIT_SKIP_MCP_MUTATION_CLASS=1 git commit ...
if [ "${SPECKIT_SKIP_MCP_MUTATION_CLASS:-0}" != "1" ]; then
  MUTCLASS_GUARD="${REPO_ROOT}/.opencode/commands/doctor/scripts/check-mcp-mutation-class.sh"
  if [ -f "$MUTCLASS_GUARD" ] && git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACM \
       | grep -Eq '^\.opencode/(skills/mcp-[a-z-]+/(scripts/(doctor|install)[a-z.-]*\.sh|mcp-servers/[^/]+/setup\.sh)|commands/doctor/assets/doctor_mcp_install\.yaml)$'; then
    if ! bash "$MUTCLASS_GUARD" "$REPO_ROOT"; then
      echo "" >&2
      echo "BLOCKED: MCP mutation-class contract failed (see FAIL lines above)." >&2
      echo "Fix: a read-only doctor must not run mutations or unbounded network calls;" >&2
      echo "     declare each script's mutation_class in doctor_mcp_install.yaml." >&2
      echo "Bypass: SPECKIT_SKIP_MCP_MUTATION_CLASS=1 git commit ..." >&2
      exit 1
    fi
  fi
fi

# --- Tool ownership map gate (blocking) ---
# Blocks commits when the committed generated map no longer matches TOOL_DEFINITIONS.
# Bypass: SPECKIT_SKIP_TOOL_OWNERSHIP_LINT=1 git commit ...
if [ "${SPECKIT_SKIP_TOOL_OWNERSHIP_LINT:-0}" != "1" ]; then
  OWNERSHIP_LINT="${REPO_ROOT}/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/tool-ownership-lint-runner.mjs"
  if [ ! -f "$OWNERSHIP_LINT" ]; then
    echo "BLOCKED: tool-ownership lint runner missing." >&2
    exit 1
  fi
  if ! node "$OWNERSHIP_LINT" >/dev/null; then
    echo "" >&2
    echo "BLOCKED: tool-ownership drift detected." >&2
    echo "Fix: regenerate mcp_server/tests/fixtures/tool-ownership-map.json from TOOL_DEFINITIONS." >&2
    echo "Bypass: SPECKIT_SKIP_TOOL_OWNERSHIP_LINT=1 git commit ..." >&2
    exit 1
  fi
fi

exit 0
